name: KAPOSI SARCOMA
definition: multicentric low-grade malignant vascular neoplasm originating from endothelial cells of late complication of AIDS

demographics.Frequency: most common AIDS-related neoplasm (40-50%); in 51% of homosexual / bisexual
demographics.Histo: proliferation of spindle cells with numerous extravasated RBCs located in clefts
demographics.Associated with: high frequency of GI tract involvement
demographics.Location: anywhere within GI tract; often multifocal

dominant_finding: nonspecific MR: low T1 + high T2 signal intensity
distribution: anywhere within GI tract; often multifocal
discriminating_features: nonspecific MR: low T1 + high T2 signal intensity
hyperattenuating lymphadenopathy ← vascularity: peripancreatic, porta hepatis,

general: nonspecific MR: low T1 + high T2 signal intensity
Lymph node involvement (13-80%, 2nd most: hyperattenuating lymphadenopathy ← vascularity: peripancreatic, porta hepatis,
GI tract (40-50% of AIDS-related Kaposi: single / multiple polypoidal 0.5-3.0-cm subepithelial nodules / masses:
± central umbilication / ulceration (common) = "bull's-eye" / "target" lesions on BE
mass enhancement greater than adjacent mucosa ← high tumor vascularity
Liver & spleen (34% at autopsy): multiple 5-12-mm nodules hyperechoic on US, hypoattenuating on NECT/CECT
Lung (18-47% of patients with cutaneous: numerous perihilar ill-defined asymmetric coalescent clusters of consolidation ± air
small (50%) / large (28%) pulmonary nodules ← tumor proliferation extending into
moderate lymphadenopathy (10-35%, late in disease): axillary, mediastinal, hilar
